title: Pearl millet rust: present status and future research needs
abstract: The present status of rust of pearl millet (Pennisetum glaucum [P. americanum]), caused by Puccinia sp., in the semi-arid tropics is reviewed, with notes on the geographic distribution and economic importance of the disease, pathogen characteristics and host range, and control methods. Future research needs are outlined
publication: International Journal of Tropical Plant Diseases
